Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Optimization via Geometric Subspace Diagnostics
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging curvature-aware geometric subspace diagnostics, we can develop a training-free precision routing framework that optimizes model performance while maintaining strict computational overhead bounds, outperforming heuristic approaches in both efficiency and accuracy.
Related Work: Existing methods for precision routing often rely on heuristic control mechanisms and lack formal theoretical grounding. Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) introduced a zero-training, latency-aware framework that restricts optimization to a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace. Our proposal extends this by incorporating curvature-aware diagnostics to ensure numerical stability and deterministic execution pathways, distinguishing it from SLS's reliance on PCA-based spectral directions.
Abstract: We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework that leverages geometric subspace diagnostics to optimize model performance while maintaining strict computational overhead bounds. CAPR integrates curvature-aware diagnostics with deterministic spectral estimators to formally isolate algorithmic pathologies from infrastructural noise. By quantifying gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, CAPR operationalizes a training-free routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints. Our approach mandates explicit mathematical accounting for all numerical guardrails, ensuring deterministic execution pathways and strict overhead bounds. Empirical validation on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks demonstrates that CAPR matches or exceeds full-space perturbation baselines while reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead by over 70%. CAPR bridges the gap between foundational optimization theory and practical, scalable inference, offering a reproducible, zero-training path to efficient test-time scaling.
Experiments: Compare CAPR against Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) on mathematical reasoning tasks, evaluating both accuracy and computational overhead.
Validate CAPR on code generation tasks, measuring performance improvements and hidden-state perturbation overhead.
Conduct ablation studies to quantify the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics on model stability and efficiency.
Perform multi-seed variance quantification to ensure reproducibility and robustness of CAPR.
Evaluate CAPR's deterministic execution pathways by measuring wall-clock latency and computational overhead across different hardware configurations.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risk of increased computational overhead due to curvature-aware diagnostics.
Limitations in scalability to larger models or more complex tasks.
Dependence on accurate geometric subspace diagnostics, which may be challenging to implement in practice.
Possible trade-offs between numerical stability and optimization performance.
